verses.latin_vulgate: Jesus ergo ante sex dies Paschæ venit Bethaniam, ubi Lazarus fuerat mortuus, quem suscitavit Jesus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ οὖν ἰησοῦς πρὸ ἓξ ἡμερῶν τοῦ πάσχα ἦλθεν εἰς βηθανίαν ὅπου ἦν λάζαρος ὁ τεθνηκώς, ὃν ἤγειρεν ἐκ νεκρῶν
verses.king_james: Then Jesus six days before the passover came to Bethany, where Lazarus was which had been dead, whom he raised from the dead.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fecerunt autem ei cœnam ibi, et Martha ministrabat, Lazarus vero unus erat ex discumbentibus cum eo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐποίησαν οὖν αὐτῷ δεῖπνον ἐκεῖ καὶ ἡ μάρθα διηκόνει ὁ δὲ λάζαρος εἷς ἦν τῶν συνανακειμένων αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: There they made him a supper; and Martha served: but Lazarus was one of them that sat at the table with him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Maria ergo accepit libram unguenti nardi pistici pretiosi, et unxit pedes Jesu, et extersit pedes ejus capillis suis: et domus impleta est ex odore unguenti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ οὖν μαρία λαβοῦσα λίτραν μύρου νάρδου πιστικῆς πολυτίμου ἤλειψεν τοὺς πόδας τοῦ ἰησοῦ καὶ ἐξέμαξεν ταῖς θριξὶν αὐτῆς τοὺς πόδας αὐτοῦ ἡ δὲ οἰκία ἐπληρώθη ἐκ τῆς ὀσμῆς τοῦ μύρου
verses.king_james: Then took Mary a pound of ointment of spikenard, very costly, and anointed the feet of Jesus, and wiped his feet with her hair: and the house was filled with the odour of the ointment.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit ergo unus ex discipulis ejus, Judas Iscariotes, qui erat eum traditurus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει οὖν εἷς ἐκ τῶν μαθητῶν αὐτοῦ ἰούδας σίμωνος ἰσκαριώτης ὁ μέλλων αὐτὸν παραδιδόναι
verses.king_james: Then saith one of his disciples, Judas Iscariot, Simon's *son*, which should betray him,

verses.latin_vulgate: Quare hoc unguentum non veniit trecentis denariis, et datum est egenis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διατί τοῦτο τὸ μύρον οὐκ ἐπράθη τριακοσίων δηναρίων καὶ ἐδόθη πτωχοῖς
verses.king_james: Why was not this ointment sold for three hundred pence, and given to the poor?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem hoc, non quia de egenis pertinebat ad eum, sed quia fur erat, et loculos habens, ea quæ mittebantur, portabat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ τοῦτο οὐχ ὅτι περὶ τῶν πτωχῶν ἔμελεν αὐτῷ ἀλλ᾽ ὅτι κλέπτης ἦν καὶ τὸ γλωσσόκομον εἶχέν καὶ τὰ βαλλόμενα ἐβάσταζεν
verses.king_james: This he said, not that he cared for the poor; but because he was a thief, and had the bag, and bare what was put therein.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit ergo Jesus: Sinite illam ut in diem sepulturæ meæ servet illud.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν οὖν ὁ ἰησοῦς ἄφες αὐτήν εἰς τὴν ἡμέραν τοῦ ἐνταφιασμοῦ μου τετηρήκεν αὐτό
verses.king_james: Then said Jesus, Let her alone: against the day of my burying hath she kept this.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pauperes enim semper habetis vobiscum: me autem non semper habetis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοὺς πτωχοὺς γὰρ πάντοτε ἔχετε μεθ᾽ ἑαυτῶν ἐμὲ δὲ οὐ πάντοτε ἔχετε
verses.king_james: For the poor always ye have with you; but me ye have not always.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cognovit ergo turba multa ex Judæis quia illic est, et venerunt, non propter Jesum tantum, sed ut Lazarum viderent, quem suscitavit a mortuis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔγνω οὖν ὄχλος πολὺς ἐκ τῶν ἰουδαίων ὅτι ἐκεῖ ἐστιν καὶ ἦλθον οὐ διὰ τὸν ἰησοῦν μόνον ἀλλ᾽ ἵνα καὶ τὸν λάζαρον ἴδωσιν ὃν ἤγειρεν ἐκ νεκρῶν
verses.king_james: Much people of the Jews therefore knew that he was there: and they came not for Jesus' sake only, but that they might see Lazarus also, whom he had raised from the dead.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cogitaverunt autem principes sacerdotum ut et Lazarum interficerent:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐβουλεύσαντο δὲ οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς ἵνα καὶ τὸν λάζαρον ἀποκτείνωσιν
verses.king_james: But the chief priests consulted that they might put Lazarus also to death;

verses.latin_vulgate: quia multi propter illum abibant ex Judæis, et credebant in Jesum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι πολλοὶ δι᾽ αὐτὸν ὑπῆγον τῶν ἰουδαίων καὶ ἐπίστευον εἰς τὸν ἰησοῦν
verses.king_james: Because that by reason of him many of the Jews went away, and believed on Jesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: In crastinum autem, turba multa quæ venerat ad diem festum, cum audissent quia venit Jesus Jerosolymam,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῇ ἐπαύριον ὄχλος πολὺς ὁ ἐλθὼν εἰς τὴν ἑορτήν ἀκούσαντες ὅτι ἔρχεται ὁ ἰησοῦς εἰς ἱεροσόλυμα
verses.king_james: On the next day much people that were come to the feast, when they heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem,

verses.latin_vulgate: acceperunt ramos palmarum, et processerunt obviam ei, et clamabant: Hosanna, benedictus qui venit in nomine Domini, rex Israël.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔλαβον τὰ βαΐα τῶν φοινίκων καὶ ἐξῆλθον εἰς ὑπάντησιν αὐτῷ καὶ ἔκραζον, ὡσαννά εὐλογημένος ὁ ἐρχόμενος ἐν ὀνόματι κυρίου ὁ βασιλεὺς τοῦ ἰσραήλ
verses.king_james: Took branches of palm trees, and went forth to meet him, and cried, Hosanna: Blessed *is* he King of Israel that cometh in the name of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et invenit Jesus asellum, et sedit super eum, sicut scriptum est:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εὑρὼν δὲ ὁ ἰησοῦς ὀνάριον ἐκάθισεν ἐπ᾽ αὐτό καθώς ἐστιν γεγραμμένον
verses.king_james: And Jesus, when he had found a young ass, sat thereon; as it is written,

verses.latin_vulgate: Noli timere, filia Sion: ecce rex tuus venit sedens super pullum asinæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ φοβοῦ θύγατερ σιών ἰδού, ὁ βασιλεύς σου ἔρχεται καθήμενος ἐπὶ πῶλον ὄνου
verses.king_james: Fear not, daughter of Sion: behold, thy King cometh, sitting on an ass's colt.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc non cognoverunt discipuli ejus primum: sed quando glorificatus est Jesus, tunc recordati sunt quia hæc erant scripta de eo, et hæc fecerunt ei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταῦτα δὲ οὐκ ἔγνωσαν οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ τὸ πρῶτον ἀλλ᾽ ὅτε ἐδοξάσθη ὁ ἰησοῦς τότε ἐμνήσθησαν ὅτι ταῦτα ἦν ἐπ᾽ αὐτῷ γεγραμμένα καὶ ταῦτα ἐποίησαν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: These things understood not his disciples at the first: but when Jesus was glorified, then remembered they that these things were written of him, and *that* they had done these things unto him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Testimonium ergo perhibebat turba, quæ erat cum eo quando Lazarum vocavit de monumento, et suscitavit eum a mortuis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐμαρτύρει οὖν ὁ ὄχλος ὁ ὢν μετ᾽ αὐτοῦ ὅτε τὸν λάζαρον ἐφώνησεν ἐκ τοῦ μνημείου καὶ ἤγειρεν αὐτὸν ἐκ νεκρῶν
verses.king_james: The people therefore that was with him when he called Lazarus out of his grave, and raised him from the dead, bare record.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propterea et obviam venit ei turba: quia audierunt fecisse hoc signum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὰ τοῦτο καὶ ὑπήντησεν αὐτῷ ὁ ὄχλος ὅτι ἤκουσεν τοῦτο αὐτὸν πεποιηκέναι τὸ σημεῖον
verses.king_james: For this cause the people also met him, for that they heard that he had done this miracle.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pharisæi ergo dixerunt ad semetipsos: Videtis quia nihil proficimus? ecce mundus totus post eum abiit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ οὖν φαρισαῖοι εἶπον πρὸς ἑαυτούς θεωρεῖτε ὅτι οὐκ ὠφελεῖτε οὐδέν ἴδε ὁ κόσμος ὀπίσω αὐτοῦ ἀπῆλθεν
verses.king_james: The Pharisees therefore said among themselves, Perceive ye how ye prevail nothing? behold, the world is gone after him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erant autem quidam gentiles, ex his qui ascenderant ut adorarent in die festo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦσαν δὲ τινες ἕλληνές ἐκ τῶν ἀναβαινόντων ἵνα προσκυνήσωσιν ἐν τῇ ἑορτῇ
verses.king_james: And there were certain Greeks among them that came up to worship at the feast:

verses.latin_vulgate: Hi ergo accesserunt ad Philippum, qui erat a Bethsaida Galilææ, et rogabant eum, dicentes: Domine, volumus Jesum videre.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗτοι οὖν προσῆλθον φιλίππῳ τῷ ἀπὸ βηθσαϊδὰ τῆς γαλιλαίας καὶ ἠρώτων αὐτὸν λέγοντες κύριε θέλομεν τὸν ἰησοῦν ἰδεῖν
verses.king_james: The same came therefore to Philip, which was of Bethsaida of Galilee, and desired him, saying, Sir, we would see Jesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit Philippus, et dicit Andreæ; Andreas rursum et Philippus dixerunt Jesu.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔρχεται φίλιππος καὶ λέγει τῷ ἀνδρέᾳ καὶ πάλιν ἀνδρέας καὶ φίλιππος λέγουσιν τῷ ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: Philip cometh and telleth Andrew: and again Andrew and Philip tell Jesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jesus autem respondit eis, dicens: Venit hora, ut clarificetur Filius hominis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἰησοῦς ἀπεκρίνατο αὐτοῖς λέγων, ἐλήλυθεν ἡ ὥρα ἵνα δοξασθῇ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου
verses.king_james: And Jesus answered them, saying, The hour is come, that the Son of man should be glorified.

verses.latin_vulgate: Amen, amen dico vobis, nisi granum frumenti cadens in terram, mortuum fuerit, ipsum solum manet: si autem mortuum fuerit, multum fructum affert.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀμὴν ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ἐὰν μὴ ὁ κόκκος τοῦ σίτου πεσὼν εἰς τὴν γῆν ἀποθάνῃ αὐτὸς μόνος μένει ἐὰν δὲ ἀποθάνῃ πολὺν καρπὸν φέρει
verses.king_james: Verily, verily, I say unto you, Except a corn of wheat fall into the ground and die, it abideth alone: but if it die, it bringeth forth much fruit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui amat animam suam, perdet eam; et qui odit animam suam in hoc mundo, in vitam æternam custodit eam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ φιλῶν τὴν ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ ἀπολέσει αὐτήν καὶ ὁ μισῶν τὴν ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ ἐν τῷ κόσμῳ τούτῳ εἰς ζωὴν αἰώνιον φυλάξει αὐτήν
verses.king_james: He that loveth his life shall lose it; and he that hateth his life in this world shall keep it unto life eternal.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si quis mihi ministrat, me sequatur, et ubi sum ego, illic et minister meus erit. Si quis mihi ministraverit, honorificabit eum Pater meus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν ἐμοί διακονῇ τις ἐμοὶ ἀκολουθείτω καὶ ὅπου εἰμὶ ἐγὼ ἐκεῖ καὶ ὁ διάκονος ὁ ἐμὸς ἔσται καὶ ἐάν τις ἐμοὶ διακονῇ τιμήσει αὐτὸν ὁ πατήρ
verses.king_james: If any man serve me, let him follow me; and where I am, there shall also my servant be: if any man serve me, him will *my* Father honour.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc anima mea turbata est. Et quid dicam? Pater, salvifica me ex hac hora. Sed propterea veni in horam hanc:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: νῦν ἡ ψυχή μου τετάρακται καὶ τί εἴπω πάτερ σῶσόν με ἐκ τῆς ὥρας ταύτης ἀλλὰ διὰ τοῦτο ἦλθον εἰς τὴν ὥραν ταύτην
verses.king_james: Now is my soul troubled; and what shall I say? Father, save me from this hour: but for this cause came I unto this hour.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pater, clarifica nomen tuum. Venit ergo vox de cælo: Et clarificavi, et iterum clarificabo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάτερ δόξασόν σου τὸ ὄνομα ἦλθεν οὖν φωνὴ ἐκ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ καὶ ἐδόξασα καὶ πάλιν δοξάσω
verses.king_james: Father, glorify thy name. Then came there a voice from heaven, *saying*, I have both glorified *it*, and will glorify *it* again.

verses.latin_vulgate: Turba ergo, quæ stabat, et audierat, dicebat tonitruum esse factum. Alii dicebant: Angelus ei locutus est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ οὖν ὄχλος ὁ ἑστὼς καὶ ἀκούσας ἔλεγεν βροντὴν γεγονέναι ἄλλοι ἔλεγον ἄγγελος αὐτῷ λελάληκεν
verses.king_james: The people therefore, that stood by, and heard *it*, said that it thundered: others said, An angel spake to him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Jesus, et dixit: Non propter me hæc vox venit, sed propter vos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη ὁ ἰησοῦς καὶ εἶπεν οὐ δι᾽ ἐμὲ αὕτη ἡ φωνὴ γέγονεν ἀλλὰ δι᾽ ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: Jesus answered and said, This voice came not because of me, but for your sakes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc judicium est mundi: nunc princeps hujus mundi ejicietur foras.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: νῦν κρίσις ἐστὶν τοῦ κόσμου τούτου νῦν ὁ ἄρχων τοῦ κόσμου τούτου ἐκβληθήσεται ἔξω
verses.king_james: Now is the judgment of this world: now shall the prince of this world be cast out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ego, si exaltatus fuero a terra, omnia traham ad meipsum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κἀγὼ ἐὰν ὑψωθῶ ἐκ τῆς γῆς πάντας ἑλκύσω πρὸς ἐμαυτόν
verses.king_james: And I, if I be lifted up from the earth, will draw all *men* unto me.

verses.latin_vulgate: (Hoc autem dicebat, significans qua morte esset moriturus.)
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦτο δὲ ἔλεγεν σημαίνων ποίῳ θανάτῳ ἤμελλεν ἀποθνῄσκειν
verses.king_james: This he said, signifying what death he should die.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit ei turba: Nos audivimus ex lege, quia Christus manet in æternum: et quomodo tu dicis: Oportet exaltari Filium hominis? quis est iste Filius hominis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη αὐτῷ ὁ ὄχλος ἡμεῖς ἠκούσαμεν ἐκ τοῦ νόμου ὅτι ὁ χριστὸς μένει εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα καὶ πῶς σὺ λέγεις ὅτι δεῖ ὑψωθῆναι τὸν υἱὸν τοῦ ἀνθρώπου τίς ἐστιν οὗτος ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου
verses.king_james: The people answered him, We have heard out of the law that Christ abideth for ever: and how sayest thou, The Son of man must be lifted up? who is this Son of man?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit ergo eis Jesus: Adhuc modicum, lumen in vobis est. Ambulate dum lucem habetis, ut non vos tenebræ comprehendant; et qui ambulat in tenebris, nescit quo vadat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν οὖν αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς ἔτι μικρὸν χρόνον τὸ φῶς μεθ᾽ ὑμῶν ἐστιν περιπατεῖτε ἕως τὸ φῶς ἔχετε ἵνα μὴ σκοτία ὑμᾶς καταλάβῃ καὶ ὁ περιπατῶν ἐν τῇ σκοτίᾳ οὐκ οἶδεν ποῦ ὑπάγει
verses.king_james: Then Jesus said unto them, Yet a little while is the light with you. Walk while ye have the light, lest darkness come upon you: for he that walketh in darkness knoweth not whither he goeth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dum lucem habetis, credite in lucem, ut filii lucis sitis. Hæc locutus est Jesus, et abiit et abscondit se ab eis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἕως τὸ φῶς ἔχετε πιστεύετε εἰς τὸ φῶς ἵνα υἱοὶ φωτὸς γένησθε ταῦτα ἐλάλησεν ὁ ἰησοῦς καὶ ἀπελθὼν ἐκρύβη ἀπ᾽ αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: While ye have light, believe in the light, that ye may be the children of light. These things spake Jesus, and departed, and did hide himself from them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem tanta signa fecisset coram eis, non credebant in eum;
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοσαῦτα δὲ αὐτοῦ σημεῖα πεποιηκότος ἔμπροσθεν αὐτῶν οὐκ ἐπίστευον εἰς αὐτόν
verses.king_james: But though he had done so many miracles before them, yet they believed not on him:

verses.latin_vulgate: ut sermo Isaiæ prophetæ impleretur, quem dixit: [Domine, quis credidit auditui nostro? et brachium Domini cui revelatum est?]
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα ὁ λόγος ἠσαΐου τοῦ προφήτου πληρωθῇ ὃν εἶπεν κύριε τίς ἐπίστευσεν τῇ ἀκοῇ ἡμῶν καὶ ὁ βραχίων κυρίου τίνι ἀπεκαλύφθη
verses.king_james: That the saying of Esaias the prophet might be fulfilled, which he spake, Lord, who hath believed our report? and to whom hath the arm of the Lord been revealed?

verses.latin_vulgate: Propterea non poterant credere, quia iterum dixit Isaias:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὰ τοῦτο οὐκ ἠδύναντο πιστεύειν ὅτι πάλιν εἶπεν ἠσαΐας
verses.king_james: Therefore they could not believe, because that Esaias said again,

verses.latin_vulgate: [Excæcavit oculos eorum, et induravit cor eorum ut non videant oculis, et non intelligant corde, et convertantur, et sanem eos.]
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τετύφλωκεν αὐτῶν τοὺς ὀφθαλμοὺς καὶ πεπώρωκεν αὐτῶν τὴν καρδίαν ἵνα μὴ ἴδωσιν τοῖς ὀφθαλμοῖς καὶ νοήσωσιν τῇ καρδίᾳ καὶ ἐπιστραφῶσιν καὶ ἰάσωμαι αὐτούς
verses.king_james: He hath blinded their eyes, and hardened their heart; that they should not see with *their* eyes, nor understand with *their* heart, and be converted, and I should heal them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc dixit Isaias, quando vidit gloriam ejus, et locutus est de eo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταῦτα εἶπεν ἠσαΐας ὅτε εἶδεν τὴν δόξαν αὐτοῦ καὶ ἐλάλησεν περὶ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: These things said Esaias, when he saw his glory, and spake of him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verumtamen et ex principibus multi crediderunt in eum: sed propter pharisæos non confitebantur, ut e synagoga non ejicerentur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅμως μέντοι καὶ ἐκ τῶν ἀρχόντων πολλοὶ ἐπίστευσαν εἰς αὐτόν ἀλλὰ διὰ τοὺς φαρισαίους οὐχ ὡμολόγουν ἵνα μὴ ἀποσυνάγωγοι γένωνται
verses.king_james: Nevertheless among the chief rulers also many believed on him; but because of the Pharisees they did not confess *him*, lest they should be put out of the synagogue:

verses.latin_vulgate: Dilexerunt enim gloriam hominum magis quam gloriam Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἠγάπησαν γὰρ τὴν δόξαν τῶν ἀνθρώπων μᾶλλον ἤπερ τὴν δόξαν τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: For they loved the praise of men more than the praise of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jesus autem clamavit, et dixit: Qui credit in me, non credit in me, sed in eum qui misit me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰησοῦς δὲ ἔκραξεν καὶ εἶπεν ὁ πιστεύων εἰς ἐμὲ οὐ πιστεύει εἰς ἐμὲ ἀλλ᾽ εἰς τὸν πέμψαντά με
verses.king_james: Jesus cried and said, He that believeth on me, believeth not on me, but on him that sent me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et qui videt me, videt eum qui misit me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὁ θεωρῶν ἐμὲ θεωρεῖ τὸν πέμψαντά με
verses.king_james: And he that seeth me seeth him that sent me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego lux in mundum veni, ut omnis qui credit in me, in tenebris non maneat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγὼ φῶς εἰς τὸν κόσμον ἐλήλυθα ἵνα πᾶς ὁ πιστεύων εἰς ἐμὲ ἐν τῇ σκοτίᾳ μὴ μείνῃ
verses.king_james: I am come a light into the world, that whosoever believeth on me should not abide in darkness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et si quis audierit verba mea, et non custodierit, ego non judico eum; non enim veni ut judicem mundum, sed ut salvificem mundum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐάν τίς μου ἀκούσῃ τῶν ῥημάτων καὶ μὴ πιστεύσῃ, ἐγὼ οὐ κρίνω αὐτόν οὐ γὰρ ἦλθον ἵνα κρίνω τὸν κόσμον ἀλλ᾽ ἵνα σώσω τὸν κόσμον
verses.king_james: And if any man hear my words, and believe not, I judge him not: for I came not to judge the world, but to save the world.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui spernit me et non accipit verba mea, habet qui judicet eum. Sermo quem locutus sum, ille judicabit eum in novissimo die.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ ἀθετῶν ἐμὲ καὶ μὴ λαμβάνων τὰ ῥήματά μου ἔχει τὸν κρίνοντα αὐτόν ὁ λόγος ὃν ἐλάλησα ἐκεῖνος κρινεῖ αὐτὸν ἐν τῇ ἐσχάτῃ ἡμέρᾳ
verses.king_james: He that rejecteth me, and receiveth not my words, hath one that judgeth him: the word that i have spoken, the same shall judge him in the last day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia ego ex meipso non sum locutus, sed qui misit me, Pater, ipse mihi mandatum dedit quid dicam et quid loquar.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι ἐγὼ ἐξ ἐμαυτοῦ οὐκ ἐλάλησα ἀλλ᾽ ὁ πέμψας με πατὴρ αὐτός μοι ἐντολὴν ἔδωκεν τί εἴπω καὶ τί λαλήσω
verses.king_james: For I have not spoken of myself; but the Father which sent me, he gave me a commandment, what I should say, and what I should speak.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et scio quia mandatum ejus vita æterna est: quæ ergo ego loquor, sicut dixit mihi Pater, sic loquor.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οἶδα ὅτι ἡ ἐντολὴ αὐτοῦ ζωὴ αἰώνιός ἐστιν ἃ οὖν λαλῶ ἐγὼ καθὼς εἴρηκέν μοι ὁ πατήρ οὕτως λαλῶ
verses.king_james: And I know that his commandment is life everlasting: whatsoever I speak therefore, even as the Father said unto me, so I speak.
